{
  "weights": {

    "RX.2": 1.0,

    "RX.5": 1.0
  },
  "active_set": ["R1.1", "R1.2", "R1.3", "R1.6", "RX.1", "RX.2", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The section opens with an explicit gap statement ('has not, to our knowledge, been treated rigorously') and a contribution claim (finite-precision bound via Dobrushin coefficient); the framing of what is new and why it matters is central to whether the section succeeds.",
    "R1.2": "The section's core content is a chain of formal mathematical claims — Lemma 1's backward-error derivation, Theorem 2's tightening, and the sqrt(n) improvement claim. Logical consistency between premises, intermediate steps, and conclusions is directly load-bearing.",
    "R1.3": "The section asserts that 'numerical experiments on WiC-Amb confirm the predicted scaling' but provides no inline evidence; alignment between this claim and whatever experimental data exists must be verified.",
    "R1.6": "Technical prose quality is plausibly load-bearing under a 'help me' delegation: notation density, sentence-level clarity, and the closing paragraph's rhetorical positioning all affect comprehensibility for a referee."
  },
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Should I focus primarily on verifying the mathematical and logical soundness of the arguments (Lemma 1, Theorem 2, the sqrt(n) tightening claim), or primarily on improving the prose clarity and presentation quality of the section?"
}

rationale.R1.1: The section opens with an explicit gap statement ('has not, to our knowledge, been treated rigorously') and a contribution claim (finite-precision bound via Dobrushin coefficient); the framing of what is new and why it matters is central to whether the section succeeds.
rationale.R1.2: The section's core content is a chain of formal mathematical claims — Lemma 1's backward-error derivation, Theorem 2's tightening, and the sqrt(n) improvement claim. Logical consistency between premises, intermediate steps, and conclusions is directly load-bearing.
rationale.R1.3: The section asserts that 'numerical experiments on WiC-Amb confirm the predicted scaling' but provides no inline evidence; alignment between this claim and whatever experimental data exists must be verified.
rationale.R1.6: Technical prose quality is plausibly load-bearing under a 'help me' delegation: notation density, sentence-level clarity, and the closing paragraph's rhetorical positioning all affect comprehensibility for a referee.

parsed.clarification_question: Should I focus primarily on verifying the mathematical and logical soundness of the arguments (Lemma 1, Theorem 2, the sqrt(n) tightening claim), or primarily on improving the prose clarity and presentation quality of the section?
